# Claude Lane Stack

> Multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code (v1.3.0): you talk to one AI project manager (`dev-orchestrator`), it dispatches optional worker lanes (AGY / Grok / Codex) through file-based YAML task contracts in `.agents/runs/`, accepts each task as it finishes (progressive accept via `lane-poll` + MODE=start/finish â€” no join-wait), verifies with an independent review lane (Codex Sol), and auto-merges finished code to git `main` via `wt-merge-main`. Built for solo developers. No required database or cloud service â€” plain files + plain git. MIT license.

Key facts:
- Only Claude Code is required; workers optional and auto-detected by `agents-doctor` (profiles: full â†’ claude-only).
- Task cards list `owns_paths` so parallel workers cannot touch each other's files; optional git worktrees.
- Merging to `main` is always done by the orchestrator, never the human.
- **Onboard 2.0**: dual scenario `minimal|full` + dual depth `fast|deep` (full defaults to deep forensic). Commands: `/project-onboard`, `/project-onboard deep`, shell `project-onboard`.
- **Long lanes**: Claude foreground Bash dies ~2 minutes â€” use `lane-bg` + `lane-wait` around `lane-exec` (activity-aware idle/max). See docs/LANE-EXEC.md.
- **Warm AGY/Grok sessions**: `lane-session` resumes run-scoped conversations, leases up to three parallel slots, and rotates after seven successful tasks by default. Review stays independent.
- **Language**: durable files English; chat with human may be Russian (docs/LANGUAGE.md).
- **Codex models**: GPT-5.6 Sol (review/deep/high-risk), Terra (scoped write/docs), Luna (trivia only). No 5.5.
- Author: Kirill Vechkasov (@VKirill). Release: https://github.com/VKirill/claude-lane-stack/releases/tag/v1.3.0
